title: pouteria multiflora var. quadrifida
intro: Pouteria multiflora var. quadrifida, also known as the Burtt's Pouteria, is a species of plant within the family Sapotaceae, native to Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The bark of the Burtt's Pouteria contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The plant is typically used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits, including reducing fever and promoting wound healing.
